parsed_review.summary: CP00-251 is a descriptor-only closeout pack covering the RP07.P04.M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail (8 rows) and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (2 rows), risk class A, 10 units, range RP07.P04.M05.S15-RP07.P04.M06.S02. The pack-level claim is correctly modest: manifest declares production_ready=false / implemented_pending_review and does not claim enterprise trust from local validation. Pack binding, requirements, plan pack, manifest, README, and the embedded contract descriptor are mutually consistent (deliverable distribution 4 implementation / 1 fixture / 1 test / 1 hermes_evidence / 1 claude_review / 2 ui = 10; micro-title 8/2; chains CP00-250->CP00-252, next subphase RP07.P04.M06.S03). All descriptor rows carry descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false, and the row-extra chain (CP248->CP250) supplies the per-row no-runtime/no-leak fields the validator checks (real_client_data_loaded, executes_command_runtime, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, leak_detected, writes_state_transition, unauthorized_count_leak_detected, executes_ui_runtime), all false. The new code is purely frozen-object construction with no real-data loading or runtime dispatch; validators, model tests, and the contract validator script (which deepEquals the embedded contract descriptor against the code-generated descriptor) strictly enforce coverage, descriptor shape, authority boundary, and handoff. index.js re-exports via export * so the 7 new public functions propagate without an edit. Authority boundary holds: Claude is a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob, read_only=true), claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review/evidence/receipt shapes (H07 gate, receipt_shape descriptor_only_..._no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence enumerates failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, plus missing-reference and final-approval/enterprise-trust claims; the readiness auth-preflight passed with well-formed JSON; and no stale/invalid claude-review-result.json exists to be wrongly accepted. Changed-file scope is bounded to the 7 modified source files plus the pack's own untracked docs, with the unrelated dirty files (.docx, ldip JSON) explicitly preserved and git diff --check reported clean. No P0 or P1 issues found; two P3 observations are non-blocking. As read-only reviewer I do not provide final approval, which remains a human gate.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts not yet present (expected at this hardening step)
findings.details: The manifest lists claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json under required_pack_artifacts/evidence_refs, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-251/. This is expected: the pack is at the read_only_claude_run_with_raw_capture step of the hardened_review_sequence, which precedes receipt_normalization, receipt_validation, finding_adjudication, construction_inspection, and final_closeout_validation. command-evidence.json (notes) correctly classifies closeout-pack:validate and the review:run/normalize/receipt:validate commands as post-review gated, run before commit. This is a sequencing reminder, not an implementation defect.
findings.recommendation: Before commit/closeout, generate the normalized receipt, adjudication, and construction-inspection artifacts from this accepted review and run the post-review gated commands (npm run closeout-pack:validate and git diff --check --cached). Pack closeout must not be marked complete until those artifacts exist and validate.

findings.title: Cosmetic dual-categorization in changed-file-scope.json
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-251/ (the pack's own artifact directory) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md are listed under both untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105). The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved more precisely lists only the truly unrelated files (the .docx and docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json). The dual-listing is cosmetic and does not affect scope correctness: active_pack_implementation_refs are explicitly enumerated, no unrelated dirty file is claimed as pack implementation, and git diff --check is reported clean.
findings.recommendation: Adjust the changed-file-scope generator so pack-owned artifact directories are not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, to keep the two categories disjoint. No functional change required for this pack.
